title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Runtime Contract: Dependency Injection Lifetime
summary: Handlers construct database pools and loggers per request, middleware changes response order, mutable globals leak across tests, and business rules cannot run without HTTP fixtures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo dependency injection lifetime increment in composed handler and dependency boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Dependency Injection Lifetime · Theory Dependency Injection Lifetime
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9162 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dependency Injection Lifetime contract for the supplied data.
